title: ficus infectoria var. lambertiana
intro: Ficus infectoria var. lambertiana, also known as Gedechus or Indian Jujube, is a plant species that belongs to the Ficus genus and is native to the tropical forests of Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine and has various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Ficus infectoria var. lambertiana include reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and alleviating respiratory problems, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by drying the leaves and bark, and then using them in teas, infusions, or decoctions to harness its medicinal properties.
